Lucas Dewey
Experience Contact
Back to experience

2016-2017

Lifestyle Design

Mechanical Engineer

Designed and prototyped consumer products at Lifestyle Design across molded plastic, sheet metal, electronics enclosures, and structural assemblies. Work spanned early concept development, engineering design, analysis, prototype refinement, production drawings, and running changes for products already in market.

Location Santa Barbara, CA

Highlights

  • Supported consumer product development across balance boards, swing boards, GPS beacons, sensing packages, and portable outdoor equipment.
  • Designed production-oriented parts and assemblies in SolidWorks across injection molded plastic, blow molded shells, sheet metal, tubing, and electronics enclosures.
  • Used FEA, CFD, prototype testing, and engineering drawings to validate designs and move concepts toward production.

Tools

SolidWorksFEACFDInjection moldingBlow moldingSheet metal designTubing and machined componentsElectronics enclosure designHeat sink designEngineering drawingsPrototype developmentProduction design supportClient collaborationIndustrial design collaboration

Projects

Work at Lifestyle Design

Project

Consumer product development

Designed and refined consumer products from concept through production support across molded plastic, sheet metal, and electronics-integrated assemblies.

Contribution

  • Collaborated with industrial designers and clients to translate concepts into manufacturable mechanical designs
  • Created production-oriented CAD models and engineering drawings for consumer hardware
  • Supported running changes for products already in market as requirements evolved

Result

Helped move multiple consumer products from early design concepts toward production-ready hardware.

Project

Foldable hammock stand

Developed a portable, foldable hammock stand concept for Grand Trunk Hammocks, balancing packability, load-bearing structure, and manufacturable custom components.

Contribution

  • Iterated the mechanism from a four-corner hinge layout to a center-hinge architecture to better maintain shape under load
  • Sourced tubing and developed the mechanical layout for the folding frame
  • Created part drawings for custom machined components

Result

Advanced a portable hammock stand concept through structural layout, prototype planning, and manufacturable component definition.

Project

Ambiently cooled heat sink optimization

Redesigned a passive heat sink for an NVIDIA Jetson sensing package after the existing design could not adequately dissipate the chip’s 18 W thermal load.

Contribution

  • Evaluated the original heat sink using empirical calculations and thermal design principles
  • Improved fin spacing, fin geometry, base thickness, and array size to increase passive cooling performance
  • Validated the updated direction with CFD before prototyping

Result

Reduced predicted chip temperature by approximately 7–8 ºC, bringing the sensing package within operating range.

© Lucas Dewey. Portfolio content is limited to public or non-confidential work. Confidential project details, implementation specifics, and proprietary visuals are intentionally omitted.
